instructions.guideline: Analyze content and guidelines from skill/dlstreamer-coding-agent folder
instructions.strict_rule: Programming language used in converted application must be the same as in the input one. This requirements overwrites any other language preferences. It is imperative to maintain consistency.
Before any code changes, execute a mandatory Compliance Gate: (1) extract all strict_rule items, (2) define explicit PASS/FAIL checks for each item, (3) block implementation if any check is FAIL.
instructions.guideline: Compliance Gate must explicitly validate: programming language consistency, inference-stage count parity, required bbox class coverage, and required validation artifacts.
instructions.strict_rule: Agent cannot stop conversion until all are fulfilled.

The converted application must preserve the same number of inference stages as the input application. Each inference stage (e.g., primary detector, secondary detector, classifier) must be mapped to a separate inference element in the converted pipeline. Merging multiple inference stages into one model/element is NOT allowed unless both stages in the original application use the exact same model.

Bounding boxes must be drawn for ALL object classes that have bounding boxes in the input application. Before selecting models, the agent must enumerate every object class that receives a bounding box in the input application (e.g., animals, persons) and ensure the converted pipeline detects and draws bboxes for each of those classes independently.
instructions.guideline: Before writing the pipeline, create an explicit "Processing Stages Inventory" that lists: (1) each inference stage in the input app, (2) what object classes it detects/classifies, (3) what visual output it produces (bbox, label, text). The converted pipeline must have a corresponding element for each row in this inventory.

When selecting replacement models, verify that the chosen model outputs the same object classes as the original. If a single replacement model covers fewer classes, add additional models to cover all original classes. Do not reduce the number of detected object types.

goal.description: Convert input application to the one which uses Deep Learning Streamer functionality
goal.guideline: Analyses content of input application: code and documentation
Collect information about deprecated API/functions from DL Streamer documentation/code and do not use them in converted application
Ensure converted application will use the same programming language as the input application. It means C to C, C++ to C++, Python to Python etc.
Try to collect input resources, like input video, the same as used by input application or very simillar to it
Add comments in the converted application which point to the same functionality in the input application
Use DL Streamer elements and plugins to achieve the same functionality as in the input application. If there is no one-to-one mapping, use combination of elements/plugins to achieve the same result
Use the same model as in the input application. If it is not possible, use the most similar one and document the difference in the documentation
If model quantization is possible to be done then do it
Create script which allow to configure environment and execute the converted application
Created scripts must contains help with information how to use it
When working with DL Streamer docker image, ensure environment variables and paths are correctly set and the latest version from hub.docker.com is used. Solve pull problem(s) if needed

validation_criteria.item: Verify an output of created sample application.
All stages of processing identified in the input application must be reflected in the converted application

The number of inference elements in the converted pipeline must equal the number of inference stages in the input application (e.g., if input has PGIE + 2×SGIE, output must have 3 separate inference elements)
Each object class that has a bounding box in the input application must also have a bounding box in the converted application output (verify visually or via metadata/JSON output)
Application must handle different input sources correctly (e.g., video file, webcam)
Converted application must maintain the same functionality as the input application, including the same object detection and recognition capabilities as well as bbox drawing for those objects
Application must compile and run without errors
Created scripts must execute without errors
Created scripts must contain help information
Output from the execution of converted application must contain detection/identification results on testing video
When using DL Streamer docker image, verify that environment variables and paths are correctly set and the latest version from hub.docker.com is used

goal.guideline: Short description of the converted application
Side-by-side diagrams comparing the DeepStream and DL Streamer pipelines
Element mapping table explaining each substitution and why the change was made
Application logic table covering probes, callbacks, metadata handling, and CLI parsing
Model comparison table showing the original model vs. the chosen replacement, format conversion steps, and rationale for the selection
Document steps to execute converted application
Describe how to download required resources (model(s), video(s))
Add chapter with information about deprecated elements

validation_criteria.item: Created documentation file
Documentation file contains short description of converted application
Documentation file contains chart with architecture comparison
Documentation file contains table with corresponding elements from input application and converted one
Documentation file contains documented steps of execution of converted application
Documentation file contains information how to download/obtain needed resources (ex. model(s), video(s))

goal.description: Quality of converted application must be acceptable

validation_criteria.item: Pipeline from converted application must detect/identify the same objects/classes on testing video and properly draw bounding boxes and labels
Verify that bounding boxes are drawn for each object class present in the input application (e.g., if input draws bbox for animals AND persons, output must draw bbox for BOTH)
Count of inference stages in converted pipeline must match the count from the input application's Processing Stages Inventory
Application must compile and run without errors
Based on generated documentation execute all steps needed to be done to execute converted application
Show final report from validation, which include statistics like: hw platform description, list od software components with versions, FPS
When using DL Streamer docker image, verify that environment variables and paths are correctly set and the latest version from hub.docker.com is used
Verify that documentation is in sync with the source code of the converted application

on_completion.summary: Short report with execution of and statistics
on_completion.compliance_gate_report: Provide Compliance Gate results with PASS/FAIL for each strict_rule, including language consistency, inference-stage parity, bbox class coverage, and validation artifacts completeness.
on_completion.compliance_gate_report_template: | Check | Status (PASS/FAIL) | Evidence | |---|---|---| | Language consistency with input application | PASS/FAIL | State input language, converted language, and file path references | | Inference-stage count parity (input vs converted) | PASS/FAIL | Provide input stage inventory count and converted element count | | BBox class coverage parity | PASS/FAIL | List classes with bbox in input and matching classes in output | | Validation artifacts completeness | PASS/FAIL | List produced artifacts: executable/script, output sample, documentation | | Overall Compliance Gate result | PASS/FAIL | PASS only if all checks above are PASS |
on_completion.diff_summary: List and description of main blocks of the converted applicaton
